Package fiveCard

Class PokerHand

java.lang.Object
fiveCard.Hand
fiveCard.PokerHand
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<Hand>
public class PokerHand extends Hand
Implements the behavior of hands in poker. fiveCard.PokerHand.java CPSC243 Spring 2026

  • Constructor Details

    • PokerHand

      public PokerHand()

  • Method Details

    • getRankArray

      public int[] getRankArray()
      Returns an int representation of the hand object, done by each card's value (rank value in particular)
      Returns:
      The int representation of the hand object, following the same order as the original hand array

    • getHandRank

      public PokerHand.HandRank getHandRank()
      Determines and returns the rank of the hand based on 5 card poker rankings
      Returns:
      An enum that represents the determined hand rank

    • top2PairCount

      public PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] top2PairCount()
      Returns an array of the 2 cards in the hand array that in terms of rank have the highest frequency. Sorted with the 1st term being the one of highest frequency
      Returns:
      An array of the top 2 cards in terms of frequency, sorted from highest to lowest card count

    • getKickerArr

      public static int[] getKickerArr(Hand hand, PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] top2Cards, int nonKickerCardCount) throws PokerException
      This function is solely for when the hand type is one that has nonkicker cards. It creates an array that is of only the kicker cards.
      Parameters:
      hand - The hand containing all of the cards
      top2Cards - Contains that cards that are considered non kicker cards (the ones avoid in the array that's returned)
      nonKickerCardCount - - Used to declare the size of the array, the amount of nonkicker cards the hand has
      Returns:
      The int representation of the passed hand, with the non kicker cards not included
      Throws:
      PokerException - If trying to get card at invalid index

    • sortTop2CardArrayByRank

      public static void sortTop2CardArrayByRank(PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] arr)
      Makes the passed array's two elements sorted from greatest to least by card rank
      Parameters:
      arr - - The array of 2 elements that we want to sort

    • kickerBreakTie

      public static PokerHand.HandResult kickerBreakTie(Hand caller, Hand dealer, PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] callerTop2Cards, PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] dealerTop2Cards) throws PokerException
      Determines the kickers of both hands and breaks the tie on the kicker cards, returning the game result.
      Parameters:
      caller - The poker hand of the caller
      dealer - The poker hand of the dealer
      callerTop2Cards - - The caller's array of their top 2 cards by card rank count (like pairs, three of a kind, etc..). Used to isolate nonkicker cards
      dealerTop2Cards - - The dealer's array of their top 2 cards by card rank count (like pairs, three of a kind, etc..). Used to isolate nonkicker cards
      Returns:
      The result of the tie break. Win and lose entail the entire hand was won or lost, push guarantees that a complete tie occured
      Throws:
      PokerException - If trying to get card at invalid index

    • breakTie

      public static PokerHand.HandResult breakTie(PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] callerTop2Cards, PokerHand.cardByCountAndRank[] dealerTop2Cards, int handRankVal)
      Breaks a tie for the case of pair based hand rankings (non kicker cards) and returns the result of this tie break
      Parameters:
      callerTop2Cards - The caller's array of their top 2 cards by card rank count (like pairs, three of a kind, etc..). Used to isolate nonkicker cards
      dealerTop2Cards - The dealer's array of their top 2 cards by card rank count (like pairs, three of a kind, etc..). Used to isolate nonkicker cards
      handRankVal - The int representation of hand rank (only used to check an edge case)
      Returns:
      The result of the break tie. Win and lose entail the entire hand was won or lost, but push means that the non kicker cards all tied and the kickers must then be considered

    • breakTie

      public static PokerHand.HandResult breakTie(int[] intCallerHand, int[] intDealerHand)
      Breaks a tie by comparing the entirety of the hands passed through by card ranks
      Parameters:
      intCallerHand - - The caller's int representation of their hand, with the ints attributed to rank value
      intDealerHand - - The dealer's int representation of their hand, with the ints attributed to rank value
      Returns:
      The result of the tie break. Win and lose entail the entire hand was won or lost, push guarantees that a complete tie occured

    • hasKickers

      public static boolean hasKickers(PokerHand.HandRank handRank)
      Determines if the handRank has kickers or not, based on poker definitions for hand ranks.
      Parameters:
      handRank - - The rank we check for kickers
      Returns:
      True if the hand rank does have kickers, false otherwise

    • isCallerWinner

      public static PokerHand.HandResult isCallerWinner(PokerHand caller, PokerHand dealer) throws PokerException
      Contains all the functionality for determining the winner of a single poker game
      Parameters:
      caller - The caller's hand, represented by the Hand object
      dealer - The dealer's hand, represented by the Hand object
      Returns:
      Returns WIN if the caller wins, LOSE if they lose, and PUSH if theres a complete tie
      Throws:
      PokerException - If trying to get card at invalid index

    • hasAceAsLowest

      public static boolean hasAceAsLowest(int[] hand, PokerHand.HandRank rankType)
      Evaluates if hand has an ace and if the ace is treated as lowest card in the hand or not
      Parameters:
      hand - The hand we insert the ace into
      rankType - The hand rank of the hand we pass through
      Returns:
      True if ace is treated as the lowest card for the given hand, false otherwise

    • insertAceToFront

      public static void insertAceToFront(int[] hand)
      Inserts an ace to the front of the hand at the lowest possible card value in place (as in doesn't return a separate array)
      Parameters:
      hand - The hand we insert the ace into

    • adjustAceValueIfNeeded

      public static void adjustAceValueIfNeeded(int[] hand, PokerHand.HandRank rankType)
      When hand contains an ace, checks if hand uses ace as lowest value and sets it to be the lowest value if so.
      Parameters:
      hand - The hand we search
      rankType - The hand rank of the hand we pass through

    • isCallerWinnerForTie

      public static PokerHand.HandResult isCallerWinnerForTie(PokerHand.HandRank rankType, PokerHand caller, PokerHand dealer) throws PokerException
      Contains all the functionality for determining the winner of a tie.
      Parameters:
      rankType - - The hand rank of the hand we pass through
      caller - - An object that is the caller's poker hand
      dealer - - An object that is the dealer's poker hand
      Returns:
      Returns WIN if the caller wins, LOSE if they lose, and PUSH if theres a complete tie
      Throws:
      PokerException - If trying to get card at invalid index

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(Hand otherHand)
      Compares PokerHands with poker scoring rules, returning the how the calling object compares to the passed object
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<Hand>
      Overrides:
      compareTo in class Hand
      Parameters:
      otherHand - The hand we are comparing the calling object with
      Returns:
      Represents how the calling object compares to the passed object. (-1 = lost, 0 = push, 1 = win)

    • comparatorIntToHandResult

      public static PokerHand.HandResult comparatorIntToHandResult(int n)
      Translates the meaning of the compareTo method for PokerHand into the HandResult enum
      Parameters:
      n - The compareTo result
      Returns:
      The HandResult representation of the compareTo value. (-1 = LOSE, 0 = PUSH, 1 = WIN)
